title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Baseline First correctness-and-cost proof for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Baseline First assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Solve a bounded debug instance with 6 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Implement a clear correct baseline before optimizing and preserve it as an oracle.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Baseline First has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
